title: Engaging men and boys in preventing family violence - Canada.ca
content:
## Engaging men and boys in preventing family violence
- Why it’s important  to engage men and boys
- Resources  for ending gender-based violence
- International  resources

## Why it’s important to engage men and boys
Engaging men and boys is critical in preventing family violence and  promoting health relationships. As partners in change, they can encourage  respect and disrupt harmful attitudes and behaviours.
This work starts with:
- understanding that family violence affects  everyone, including men and boys
- making sure that all survivors, including men and  boys, can access support to heal  from past harm and build healthy relationships
- to heal  from past harm and build healthy relationships
- recognizing that men and boys often experience pressure  to be tough which can  make it hard to talk about feelings, build safe, healthy relationships or ask  for help
- which can  make it hard to talk about feelings, build safe, healthy relationships or ask  for help
- creating safe, supportive communities for men and  boys places  where they can connect, learn from each other and take positive action
- places  where they can connect, learn from each other and take positive action
- equipping men and boys with tools to recognize and challenge  harmful or violent attitudes and behaviours online,  in locker rooms, in classrooms or in their communities
- online,  in locker rooms, in classrooms or in their communities
By joining the conversation and speaking out against violence, men and  boys:
- model positive behaviours
- help create safe, healthy relationships
- advance gender equality at home, in schools, in  workplaces and beyond

## Resources  for ending gender-based violence
The following  organizations focus on engaging men and boys in preventing family violence and  promoting healthy relationships.

### White Ribbon: Engaging men and boys as allies to promote gender equality
White Ribbon is a global movement to end gender-based violence and  discrimination by engaging men and boys as allies.
White Ribbon works to promote healthy masculinities and advance gender  equity through education, awareness-raising, outreach and training. Resources  include campaigns, tools, strategies and reports.

### Shift: The project to end domestic violence
Shift works to prevent gender-based violence by turning knowledge into  action. This entails research, learning from communities and partners, and  working directly with men in male-dominated fields.
Shift has developed resources on how to engage and mobilize men and boys  in preventing gender-based violence and promoting gender equality.

### Next Gen  Men: Changing the way we see, act and think about masculinity
Next Gen Men promotes positive masculinities, improves men’s mental  health and advances gender equity through programs, community education and  workplace initiatives.
Resources include reports, guides and toolkits for educators, parents  and service providers.

## International resources
The following international  organizations focus on engaging men and boys in preventing family violence and  promoting healthy relationships.

### OurWatch: Masculinity and violence  against women
OurWatch is an  Australian organization that focuses on preventing violence against women. Its  work includes turning research findings into accessible resources and tools for  practitioners who engage men and boys in preventing violence and promoting  healthy masculinity.

### Wales Without Violence
Wales Without  Violence is a framework that focuses on supporting professionals to end  violence among children and young people. This resource offers an  evidence-informed toolkit. The toolkit contains information for understanding,  supporting and assessing the role that programs engaging men and boys can play  in preventing violence.

### Futures Without Violence
Futures Without  Violence provides programs, policies and campaigns that support individuals and  organizations working to end violence against women and children around the  world. In partnership with the U.S. Department of Justice, this organization  created Engaging Men as Leaders to End Violence: A National Strategy Center.  The Center is a consolidated hub to learn and share programs and best practices  that support the engagement of men as allies and partners in efforts to end  violence.
